title: centaurea scabiosa subsp. apiculata
intro: Centaurea scabiosa subsp. apiculata is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sunflower family (Asteraceae), commonly known as scabious, characterized by its vibrant blue or purple flowers and hairy leaves. Traditionally, Centaurea scabiosa subsp. apiculata has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of alkaloids and flavonoids. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in the treatment of skin conditions and wounds, due to its antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Centaurea scabiosa subsp. apiculata is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used topically in ointments and creams, to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
